hypothesis: Incorporating temperature-dependent molecular conformational dynamics into foundation model pre-training—by augmenting SMILES corpora with thermally-sampled 3D conformer ensembles and their associated Boltzmann weights—will enable electrolyte ionic conductivity predictions to achieve lower mean absolute error than current SMILES-only foundation models across a temperature range of −40°C to 80°C, particularly for formulations containing flexible ether-based solvents where room-temperature static representations fail to capture entropy-driven solvation shell reorganization that governs ion transport at extreme temperatures.
